issues.title: timestamp
issues.description:
Crowdfunding.refund() (contract.sol#102-113) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- block.timestamp <= deadline (contract.sol#103)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: timestamp
issues.description:
Crowdfunding.contribute() (contract.sol#71-83) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- block.timestamp > deadline (contract.sol#73)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: solc-version
issues.description:
Version constraint ^0.8.20 contains known severe issues (https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bugs.html)
	- VerbatimInvalidDeduplication
	- FullInlinerNonExpressionSplitArgumentEvaluationOrder
	- MissingSideEffectsOnSelectorAccess.
It is used by:
	- ^0.8.20 (contract.sol#2)

issues.title: reentrancy-unlimited-gas
issues.description:
Reentrancy in Crowdfunding.withdrawFunds() (contract.sol#92-99):
	External calls:
	- address(owner).transfer(amount) (contract.sol#96)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- FundsWithdrawn(amount) (contract.sol#98)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: reentrancy-unlimited-gas
issues.description:
Reentrancy in Crowdfunding.refund() (contract.sol#102-113):
	External calls:
	- address(msg.sender).transfer(contributedAmount) (contract.sol#110)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- RefundIssued(msg.sender,contributedAmount) (contract.sol#112)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: Dependence on predictable environment variable (SWC 116)
issues.description:
A control flow decision is made based on The block.timestamp environment variable.
The block.timestamp environment variable is used to determine a control flow decision. Note that the values of variables like coinbase, gaslimit, block number and timestamp are predictable and can be manipulated by a malicious miner. Also keep in mind that attackers know hashes of earlier blocks. Don't use any of those environment variables as sources of randomness and be aware that use of these variables introduces a certain level of trust into miners.
Classification: SWC-116

stage2_metadata.security_summary:
Contract Type: Custom
Classification Confidence: 90%
Access Control: role_based
Security Features: Standard
Implementation: Custom from scratch
Subtype: crowdfunding

Reasoning: The contract involves managing contributions towards a funding goal, which does not fit any standard template like ERC20, ERC721, or Governor. It is a unique business logic for crowdfunding.

classification.reasoning: The contract involves managing contributions towards a funding goal, which does not fit any standard template like ERC20, ERC721, or Governor. It is a unique business logic for crowdfunding.
